item.title: Docker and Dockerfile
item.description: What is Docker? Docker is a platform that allows developers to build, ship, and run applications within lightweight, portable containers What is container? A container or a Docker container is a lightweight, isolated package that holds an application and everything it needs to run (code, libraries, ...

item.title: System Design
item.description: For a DevOps engineer, the core focus in system design and architecture should be on Operability and Lifecycle Management.

item.title: Basics of System Design
item.description: System Design Fundamentals (for DevOps): Understanding Requirements: Functional: What the system does (e.g., handles user login, processes payments).

item.title: Practical application of System Design
item.description: Distributed Systems Concepts: Elaboration: Modern applications are almost always distributed, meaning components run on multiple machines, often across different geographical locations.

item.title: Basics of Networking in Kubernetes
item.description: Kubernetes networking is the system that allows containers/pods on the same node or different nodes in a Kubernetes cluster to communicate with each other.

item.title: DevOps Learning Perspective
item.description: “In every task what you do, it’s not about only implementation, but most importantly its about learning on high-level, in a bigger system what exactly your part is, try to understand the concept and develop a bird eye understanding of your task.

item.title: Environments
item.description: Non-Prod Envs PaaS DEV: Playground for Developers, DevOps, etc peoples.

item.title: Azure Services
item.description: Popular Azure Services Compute Services Azure Virtual Machines (VMs): Description: Allows you to create and run virtual computers (servers) in the cloud.

item.title: AWS Services
item.description: Popular AWS Services Compute Services Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud): Description: This is like renting virtual computers (servers) in the cloud.
